title: Lightly Shattering Thai Coconut Pad Thai
description: A pungent, shattering Thai dish featuring desiccated coconut and a whole30-friendly mother sauce base, reminiscent of Southern Thai cuisine.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the shrimp and cook until pink, about 2 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the peanuts and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the tamarind paste, fish sauce, coconut milk, soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger, and shrimp paste, and stir to combine.
Add the zucchini, red bell pepper, and bean sprouts, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the cooked shrimp, kaffir lime leaves, galangal,Thai basil, and fried shallots, and stir to combine.
Add the desiccated coconut and stir-fry for about 2 minutes, until lightly browned.
Add the sambal oelek and stir-fry for another minute.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the Pad Thai to a serving platter and garnish with cilantro, roasted peanuts, and a sprinkle of desiccated coconut.
Serve with fresh lime wedges on the side.
Combine the lime juice and palm sugar in a small bowl, and serve as a sauce on the side.
Garnish with additional cilantro and peanuts if desired.
Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the egg whites and cook until lightly scrambled, about 2 minutes.
Add the sliced Thai chilies and cook until slightly softened, about 1 minute.
Add the cooked egg mixture to the Pad Thai and stir to combine.
Serve immediately.
Heat the sambal oelek and shrimp paste in a small pan over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and stir to combine.
Add 2 tablespoons of water and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook until slightly thickened, about 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Use as a sauce or garnish.
Combine the kaffir lime leaves, galangal, Thai basil, and cilantro in a small bowl.
Mince the mixture and use as a garnish or sauce.
Cook the bean sprouts in a small pan of boiling water until slightly tender, about 3-5 minutes.
Shock in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Drain and chop the bean sprouts and add to the Pad Thai.
Peel and slice the Thai chilies and add to the Pad Thai.
Heat the vegetable oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the sliced Thai chilies and cook until slightly softened, about 1 minute.
Add the cooked chilies to the Pad Thai and stir to combine.
